summary: Successfully tested 'Scoperta delle Stelle' (Star Discovery) system. All 20 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend Cinema Journal loads correctly. Backend endpoints /api/cinema-news and /api/discovered-stars return 200 OK with proper data structures. Hidden Gem detection is correctly implemented (criteria: unknown fame + 4+ stars). Currently no Hidden Gems or discovered stars in database, which is expected data state.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/cinema-news returns 200 with news array
Backend: GET /api/cinema-news accepts limit parameter
Backend: News items have title_localized and content_localized fields
Backend: GET /api/discovered-stars returns 200 with stars array
Backend: Discovered stars have proper structure (id, name, is_discovered_star)
Backend: All actors have is_hidden_gem field
Backend: All actors have star_potential field (numeric)
Backend: Hidden gem criteria is correctly enforced (unknown fame + stars >= 4)
Backend: All actors have valid fame_category (unknown/rising/known/famous/superstar)
Backend: All actors have stars rating (1-5)
Backend: Directors have is_hidden_gem and star_potential fields
Backend: Screenwriters have is_hidden_gem and star_potential fields
Backend: Actors have discovered_by field
Backend: GET /api/films/cinema-journal returns 200 with films array
Frontend: Cinema Journal page loads without errors
Frontend: Cinema Journal shows films ranked by quality
Frontend: Breaking News section is conditionally hidden (no news in DB - correct behavior)
Frontend: Hall of Fame section is conditionally hidden (no discovered stars - correct behavior)
Frontend: Cast selection shows fame badges (Conosciuto, Famoso, Emergente)
Frontend: Cast selection shows star ratings and experience years
critical_code_review_comments: Star Discovery system is well implemented - check_star_discovery() function creates news in cinema_news collection correctly
Discovery rewards user with $500K, +5 likeability, +200 XP as specified
Hidden Gem indicator (Sparkles icon) is in frontend code but no matching actors exist currently
context_for_next_testing_agent: Star Discovery system verified working: 1) GET /api/cinema-news returns news array (currently empty - no discoveries yet), 2) GET /api/discovered-stars returns stars array (currently empty), 3) All actors/directors/screenwriters have is_hidden_gem, star_potential, discovered_by fields, 4) Hidden Gem criteria: fame_category='unknown' AND stars>=4 (currently no actors meet this criteria in the database), 5) Frontend Cinema Journal conditionally shows Breaking News and Hall of Fame sections only when data exists. To test full discovery flow would require creating a film with a Hidden Gem actor achieving high quality score.

cinema_news_endpoint.details: GET /api/cinema-news returns 200 with news array, supports limit parameter, localizes titles/content

discovered_stars_endpoint.details: GET /api/discovered-stars returns 200 with stars array, includes discoverer details

hidden_gem_detection.details: is_hidden_gem field present on all cast, criteria correctly enforced (unknown fame + 4+ stars)

star_potential_field.details: star_potential field present on unknown/rising cast members

cinema_journal_frontend.details: Page loads, shows films, Breaking News/Hall of Fame sections conditionally visible

cast_selection_ui.details: Fame badges (Conosciuto, Famoso, Emergente), star ratings, experience years all displayed

note_on_hidden_gems: Currently no Hidden Gem actors exist in the database because the criteria (unknown fame + 4+ stars) is rare. The unknown actors have 2 stars, not 4+. This is valid data state - hidden gems would need to be generated with the right combination of attributes.
